name: Sha Wujing
description: Sha Wujing, also known as Sandy, is one of the four main characters in the classic Chinese novel *Journey to the West*. He was originally a celestial general named Marshal Canopy, but was banished to the mortal realm after accidentally breaking a crystal goblet during a Peach Banquet. He became a fearsome river monster, living in the Flowing Sand River and preying on travelers. Guanyin, the Bodhisattva of Compassion, recruited him to become a disciple of the Tang Monk Xuanzang on his pilgrimage to the West to retrieve Buddhist scriptures. As part of his penance, Sha Wujing vowed to protect the monk and help him reach his destination. He is depicted as a strong, reliable, and loyal member of the group, known for his quiet demeanor and unwavering dedication to his task. He carries a crescent-moon shovel (also known as a monk's spade or zen stick) as his weapon. While not as magically powerful as Sun Wukong or Zhu Bajie, he is a formidable fighter and provides essential support to the group. He is often portrayed as the most level-headed and dutiful of the three disciples. His significance lies in his representation of repentance, redemption, and the importance of unwavering commitment to a righteous cause. He embodies the ideal of a steadfast and dependable follower, essential for the success of any challenging endeavor. Sha Wujing's journey from a fearsome monster to a devoted disciple highlights the transformative power of compassion and the potential for redemption within everyone.
subtitle: The Steadfast and Loyal Disciple
achievements: Became a disciple of Tang Sanzang
Protected Tang Sanzang on the Journey to the West

enemies.name: Various demons and monsters encountered on the journey

keyMyths.title: Becoming a Disciple
keyMyths.description: Sha Wujing was recruited by Guanyin to serve as a disciple of Tang Sanzang as penance for his past sins. He vowed to protect the monk and help him reach the Western Paradise. This marked his transformation from a river monster to a devoted follower.

keyMyths.title: Battles in the Flowing Sand River
keyMyths.description: Prior to becoming a disciple, Sha Wujing terrorized travelers in the Flowing Sand River. He battled Sun Wukong and Zhu Bajie before being subdued and accepting his role in the pilgrimage. These battles showcased his formidable strength.

keyMyths.title: Loyalty to Tang Sanzang
keyMyths.description: Throughout the journey, Sha Wujing remained steadfastly loyal to Tang Sanzang, protecting him from danger and assisting in overcoming obstacles. His unwavering devotion was crucial to the success of the pilgrimage.

sources.author: Wu Cheng'en
sources.description: The primary source for the story of Sha Wujing.
